expectations.text: Names SRP and frames it as 'one actor' (not 'one reason to change')
expectations.evidence: Uses the older 'one reason to change' formulation that the skill explicitly warns against: "SRP (Martin's formulation) says a module should have one reason to change"

expectations.text: Identifies the distinct actors (5 stakeholder groups)
expectations.evidence: Table: Finance / Marketing / Warehouse / DBA / Accounting

expectations.text: Proposes concrete split into OrderTotalCalculator / InventoryUpdater / ConfirmationNotifier / OrderRepository / InvoicePresenter
expectations.evidence: PricingCalculator, OrderRepository, InventoryService, OrderNotifier, InvoiceRenderer — close match
Mentions 'merge conflicts' generically but does not name the accidental-duplication trap (shared helper drifting between actors)

expectations.text: Mentions Facade as optional preservation of a single entry point
expectations.evidence: Keeps OrderService as a thin orchestrator — a different pattern, no Facade mentioned
